COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR FILED BY DEFENDANT JEUNG, JULIE
NOTICE OF INTENT TO APPEAR VIA COURTCALL (TRANSACTION ID # 60240195) FILED BY PLAINTIFF DYCK-O'NEAL, INC.

ORDER GRANTING DEFT'S MOTION TO VACATE CALIFORNIA SISTER STATE JUDGMENT AND FOR STAY OF ENFORCEMENT (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) (COPY IN SASE)

UNDER APPLICATION TO THE CLERK FOR JUDGMENT UNDER SEC 1710.25 C.C.P., IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF DYCK-O'NEAL, INC. RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT JEUNG, JULIE $208,264.30 PRINCIPAL, $13,279.00 INTEREST, $450.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $221,993.30, SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 100003395) PER ORDER FILED ON 3/27/17 (501) ALL FURTHER PROCESSING TO BE DONE BY DEFAULT SECTION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JULIE JEUNG'S MOTION TO VACATE CALIFORNIA SISTER-STATE JUDGMENT AND FOR STAY OF ENFORCEMENT TO BE HEARD IN DEPARTMENT 501, ON MARCH 14, 2017, AT 9:30 A.M. THAT DEPARTMENT HANDLES ALL FORECLOSURE-RELATED MATTERS PURSUANT TO SF LOCAL RULE 8.10. BECAUSE THIS ACTION RELATES TO A DEFICIENCY JUDGMENT AFTER A FORECLOSURE, IT IS A DEPARTMENT 501 CASE. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG

MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-14-2017 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT JULIE JEUNG'S MOTION TO VACATE CALIFORNIA SISTER-STATE JUDGMENT AND FOR STAY OF ENFORCEMENT IS GRANTED. MOVING PARTY MET ITS BURDEN UNDER CCP 1710.40(B) AND CANSECO MARKETING V. IFA & INS. SERVICES, INC. (2013) 221 CA.APP.4TH 831. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (D501)
DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITHOUT PREJUDICE (TRANSACTION ID # 100014079)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate California Sister-State Judgment And For Stay Of Enforcement
Off Calendar Mar-14-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JULIE JEUNG's Motion To Vacate California Sister-State Judgment And For Stay Of Enforcement is GRANTED. Moving party met its burden under CCP 1710.40(b) and Canseco Marketing v. IFA & Ins. Services, Inc. (2013) 221 Ca.App.4th 831. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: A. James Robertson II, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate California Sister-State Judgment And For Stay Of Enforcement
Off Calendar Mar-07-2017 Continued To Mar-14-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JULIE JEUNG Motion To Vacate California Sister-State Judgment And For Stay Of Enforcement to be heard in Department 501, on March 14, 2017, at 9:30 a.m. That department handles all foreclosure-related matters pursuant to SF Local Rule 8.10. Because this action relates to a deficiency judgment after a foreclosure, it is a Department 501 case. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
